records.ety: [AS. gor dirt, dung; akin to Icel. gor, SW. gorr, OHG. gor, and perh. to E. cord, chord, and yarn; cf. Icel. görn, garnir, guts.]

defs: Blood; especially, blood that after effusion has become thick or clotted.

records.ety: [OE. gore, gare, AS. g<?/ra angular point of land, fr. g<?/r spear; akin to D. geer gore, G. gehre gore, ger spear, Icel. geiri gore, geir spear, and prob. to E. goad. Cf. Gar, n., Garlic, and Gore, v.]

defs: A wedgeshaped or triangular piece of cloth, canvas, etc., sewed into a garment, sail, etc., to give greater width at a particular part.
A small triangular piece of land.
One of the abatements. It is made of two curved lines, meeting in an acute angle in the fesse point.
notes: ☞ It is usually on the sinister side, and of the tincture called tenn. Like the other abatements it is a modern fancy and not actually used.

records.ety: [OE. gar spear, AS. g<?/r. See 2d Gore.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Gored (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Goring.]

defs: To pierce or wound, as with a horn; to penetrate with a pointed instrument, as a spear; to stab.
quotes: The low stumps shall gore His daintly feet.

defs: To cut in a triangular form; to piece with a gore; to provide with a gore; as, to gore an apron.
